result:
Build is green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `findfile` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/loadlib.rs:613` had a leftover Phase-A `TODO(port)` that swallowed the `'package.path' must be a string` error when `package.path` was set to a non-string value (e.g. a table). It popped the nil and returned `Ok(None)`, which let `searcher_lua` continue and produced a generic "module not found" message that did not contain the substring `"package.path"` — failing the assertion at attrib.lua line 46 (`assert(not s and string.find(err, "package.path"))`).

**Fix:** Replaced the silent `Ok(None)` with `Err(LuaError::runtime("'package.<pname>' must be a string"))`, matching the C `luaL_error` in `loadlib.c findfile`. The signature was already `Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, LuaError>`, so the stale comment was wrong — no signature change was needed.

**State after fix:** Compile is green (`cargo build -q -p lua-cli` produces no errors, only pre-existing warnings about unused `Result`s elsewhere). I did not re-execute attrib.lua per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-test. The next time `pcall(require, "no-such-file")` runs with `package.path = {}`, `err` will contain the string `'package.path' must be a string`, which contains `"package.path"`, so the assertion should now pass.
